<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en-GB">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
<title>NYC Restaurant Inspection Analysis: Findings Dashboard</title>
<style>
  :root{
    --ink:#13233b;
    --ink-soft:#3a4d68;
    --paper:#f6f3ec;
    --paper-2:#fffdf8;
    --line:#d9d2c4;
    --amber:#c8741a;
    --amber-soft:#e9b773;
    --shadow:rgba(19,35,59,.10);
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box;}
  html{scroll-behavior:smooth;}
  body{
    margin:0;background:var(--paper);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:"Inter","Helvetica Neue",Arial,sans-serif;
    font-size:16px;line-height:1.6;-webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased;
  }
  .wrap{max-width:1180px;margin:0 auto;padding:0 28px;}

  header.mast{border-bottom:2px solid var(--ink);background:var(--paper-2);padding:46px 0 30px;}
  .eyebrow{font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",ui-monospace,Menlo,monospace;font-size:12px;letter-spacing:.22em;
    text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--amber);margin:0 0 14px;font-weight:600;}
  h1.title{font-family:Georgia,"Times New Roman",serif;font-weight:600;font-size:clamp(30px,5vw,52px);
    line-height:1.05;margin:0 0 16px;letter-spacing:-.01em;}
  .sub{font-size:clamp(15px,2vw,18px);color:var(--ink-soft);max-width:60ch;margin:0;}
  .provenance{margin-top:24px;display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:0;border-top:1px solid var(--line);}
  .prov-cell{flex:1 1 200px;padding:14px 20px 4px 0;border-right:1px solid var(--line);margin-right:20px;}
  .prov-cell:last-child{border-right:none;}
  .prov-num{font-family:Georgia,serif;font-size:26px;font-weight:600;display:block;line-height:1.1;}
  .prov-lab{font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",monospace;font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.12em;
    text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--ink-soft);}

  nav.jump{position:sticky;top:0;z-index:40;background:var(--ink);border-bottom:1px solid #0d1828;}
  nav.jump ul{max-width:1180px;margin:0 auto;padding:0 28px;list-style:none;display:flex;gap:0;flex-wrap:wrap;}
  nav.jump a{display:block;padding:14px 22px 14px 0;margin-right:22px;color:#cdd6e4;text-decoration:none;
    font-size:13.5px;font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",monospace;letter-spacing:.04em;
    border-bottom:2px solid transparent;transition:color .18s,border-color .18s;}
  nav.jump a:hover,nav.jump a:focus-visible{color:#fff;border-bottom-color:var(--amber);}
  nav.jump a b{color:var(--amber-soft);font-weight:600;margin-right:8px;}

  section.q{padding:64px 0;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);scroll-margin-top:60px;}
  .q-head{display:flex;gap:24px;align-items:flex-start;margin-bottom:10px;}
  .q-index{font-family:Georgia,serif;font-size:clamp(40px,7vw,72px);line-height:.9;color:var(--amber);
    font-weight:600;flex:0 0 auto;}
  .q-headtext{flex:1 1 auto;}
  .q-kicker{font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",monospace;font-size:11.5px;letter-spacing:.2em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--ink-soft);margin:6px 0 8px;}
  h2.q-title{font-family:Georgia,serif;font-weight:600;font-size:clamp(21px,3.2vw,30px);line-height:1.18;
    margin:0;letter-spacing:-.01em;max-width:30ch;}
  .finding{margin:22px 0 30px;padding:20px 24px;background:var(--paper-2);border-left:3px solid var(--amber);
    font-size:16.5px;color:var(--ink);max-width:78ch;box-shadow:0 1px 0 var(--shadow);}
  .finding strong{color:var(--ink);}
  .finding .tag{display:inline-block;font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",monospace;font-size:10.5px;letter-spacing:.14em;
    text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--amber);margin-bottom:8px;font-weight:600;}
  .finding .note{display:block;margin-top:12px;font-size:14px;color:var(--ink-soft);font-style:italic;}

  figure{margin:0;}
  .fig-frame{background:var(--paper-2);border:1px solid var(--line);padding:14px;box-shadow:0 6px 22px var(--shadow);}
  .fig-frame img{display:block;width:100%;height:auto;cursor:zoom-in;}
  figcaption{margin-top:12px;font-size:13px;color:var(--ink-soft);font-style:italic;max-width:80ch;}
  figcaption .fno{font-style:normal;font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",monospace;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink);
    font-size:12px;letter-spacing:.06em;margin-right:6px;}

  /* Violation decode table */
  .decode{margin-top:30px;}
  .decode h3{font-family:Georgia,serif;font-weight:600;font-size:18px;margin:0 0 6px;}
  .decode p.lead{font-size:13.5px;color:var(--ink-soft);font-style:italic;margin:0 0 14px;}
  table.codes{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;background:var(--paper-2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    box-shadow:0 6px 22px var(--shadow);font-size:14.5px;}
  table.codes thead th{background:var(--ink);color:#fff;text-align:left;padding:11px 14px;
    font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",monospace;font-size:11.5px;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;font-weight:600;}
  table.codes td{padding:11px 14px;border-top:1px solid var(--line);vertical-align:top;}
  table.codes td.code{font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",monospace;font-weight:600;color:var(--amber);
    white-space:nowrap;width:90px;}
  table.codes tbody tr:nth-child(even){background:rgba(217,210,196,.18);}

  .heat-row{display:flex;gap:18px;align-items:stretch;}
  .heat-main{flex:1 1 auto;min-width:0;}
  .heat-legend{flex:0 0 120px;background:var(--paper-2);border:1px solid var(--line);padding:14px 10px;
    display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;box-shadow:0 6px 22px var(--shadow);}
  .heat-legend img{width:auto;max-height:430px;height:100%;display:block;}
  .heat-legend .lgnd-note{font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",monospace;font-size:10px;letter-spacing:.08em;
    text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--ink-soft);text-align:center;margin-top:10px;line-height:1.5;}

  .tabs{display:flex;gap:0;flex-wrap:wrap;margin-bottom:0;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);}
  .tab-btn{appearance:none;background:none;border:none;cursor:pointer;font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",monospace;
    font-size:13px;letter-spacing:.03em;color:var(--ink-soft);padding:12px 20px;border-bottom:2px solid transparent;
    margin-bottom:-1px;transition:color .18s,border-color .18s;}
  .tab-btn:hover{color:var(--ink);}
  .tab-btn[aria-selected="true"]{color:var(--ink);border-bottom-color:var(--amber);font-weight:600;}
  .tab-panel{display:none;padding-top:22px;}
  .tab-panel.active{display:block;animation:fade .3s ease;}
  @keyframes fade{from{opacity:0;transform:translateY(4px);}to{opacity:1;transform:none;}}

  .lb{position:fixed;inset:0;background:rgba(11,18,28,.92);z-index:90;display:none;align-items:center;
    justify-content:center;padding:30px;cursor:zoom-out;}
  .lb.open{display:flex;}
  .lb img{max-width:96vw;max-height:92vh;width:auto;height:auto;box-shadow:0 10px 60px rgba(0,0,0,.6);}
  .lb-close{position:absolute;top:18px;right:24px;color:#fff;font-size:30px;font-family:Georgia,serif;
    cursor:pointer;line-height:1;background:none;border:none;}

  footer{padding:40px 0 60px;background:var(--ink);color:#c9d2e0;}
  footer .fineprint{font-size:12px;color:#7e8da3;font-family:"IBM Plex Mono",monospace;letter-spacing:.04em;}

  @media (max-width:720px){
    .heat-row{flex-direction:column;}
    .heat-legend{flex-direction:row;gap:14px;justify-content:center;padding:12px;}
    .heat-legend img{max-height:200px;}
    .q-head{gap:14px;}
    .prov-cell{margin-right:0;border-right:none;}
    table.codes td.code{width:auto;}
  }
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){html{scroll-behavior:auto;}.tab-panel.active{animation:none;}}
</style>
</head>
<body>

<header class="mast">
  <div class="wrap">
    <p class="eyebrow">Public Health Analytics · DOHMH Open Data</p>
    <h1 class="title">New York City Restaurant<br>Inspection Findings</h1>
    <p class="sub">An exploratory analysis of where food safety risk concentrates across the five boroughs, by violation type, cuisine, neighbourhood, and time. Built in MySQL and Power BI.</p>
    <div class="provenance">
      <div class="prov-cell"><span class="prov-num">285,177</span><span class="prov-lab">Inspections analysed</span></div>
      <div class="prov-cell"><span class="prov-num">2022 to 2025</span><span class="prov-lab">Period covered</span></div>
      <div class="prov-cell"><span class="prov-num">5</span><span class="prov-lab">Boroughs</span></div>
      <div class="prov-cell"><span class="prov-num">4</span><span class="prov-lab">Research questions</span></div>
    </div>
  </div>
</header>

<nav class="jump">
  <ul>
    <li><a href="#q1"><b>Q1</b>Common violations</a></li>
    <li><a href="#q2"><b>Q2</b>Cuisine &amp; neighbourhood</a></li>
    <li><a href="#q3"><b>Q3</b>Grades &amp; trends</a></li>
    <li><a href="#q4"><b>Q4</b>Where to focus</a></li>
  </ul>
</nav>

<main class="wrap">

  <!-- Q1 -->
  <section class="q" id="q1">
    <div class="q-head">
      <div class="q-index">1</div>
      <div class="q-headtext">
        <p class="q-kicker">Violation frequency · by borough</p>
        <h2 class="q-title">Which violations are most common, and where do they occur most frequently?</h2>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="finding">
      <span class="tag">Finding</span>
      Two violations dominate every borough. <strong>10F</strong> (non-food contact surface issues) and <strong>08A</strong> (conditions conducive to pests) rank first and second in all five boroughs. The codes that follow in ranks three to five vary by borough, but 10F and 08A lead everywhere, pointing to systemic citywide issues rather than localised ones.
    </div>
    <figure>
      <div class="fig-frame">
        <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="100% stacked bar chart of the top 5 violations for each borough, showing count and percentage share. 10F is the largest segment in every borough.">
      </div>
      <figcaption><span class="fno">Fig 1</span>Top 5 violations per borough by count and percentage share. Each bar is one borough, segmented by its top five violation codes; total violation counts are shown above each bar. Eight unique codes appear across the chart as the top five vary by borough.</figcaption>
    </figure>

    <div class="decode">
      <h3>Violation code reference</h3>
      <p class="lead">Descriptions for all unique violation codes appearing in the chart above.</p>
      <table class="codes">
        <thead><tr><th>Violation code</th><th>Violation description</th></tr></thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr><td class="code">02B</td><td>Hot TCS food item not held at or above 140°F.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">02G</td><td>Cold TCS food item held above 41°F; smoked or processed fish held above 38°F; intact raw eggs held above 45°F; or reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) TCS foods held above required temperatures except during active necessary preparation.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">04L</td><td>Evidence of mice or live mice in establishment's food or non-food areas.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">06C</td><td>Food, supplies, or equipment not protected from potential source of contamination during storage, preparation, transportation, display, service or from customer's refillable, reusable container. Condiments not in single-service containers or dispensed directly by the vendor.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">06D</td><td>Food contact surface not properly washed, rinsed and sanitised after each use and following any activity when contamination may have occurred.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">08A</td><td>Establishment is not free of harborage or conditions conducive to rodents, insects or other pests.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">10B</td><td>Anti-siphonage or back-flow prevention device not provided where required; equipment or floor not properly drained; sewage disposal system in disrepair or not functioning properly. Condensation or liquid waste improperly disposed of.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">10F</td><td>Non-food contact surface or equipment made of unacceptable material, not kept clean, or not properly sealed, raised, spaced or movable to allow accessibility for cleaning on all sides, above and underneath the unit.</td></tr>
        </tbody>
      </table>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- Q2 -->
  <section class="q" id="q2">
    <div class="q-head">
      <div class="q-index">2</div>
      <div class="q-headtext">
        <p class="q-kicker">Performance · cuisine × borough</p>
        <h2 class="q-title">Which cuisines and neighbourhoods have the lowest food safety performance?</h2>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="finding">
      <span class="tag">Finding</span>
      With cuisines grouped into broader categories, <strong>South Asian</strong> and <strong>African</strong> are the weakest performers by average score across several boroughs. South Asian peaks in Queens (average 37.1 across 2,415 inspections); African produces the single worst cuisine-borough score in Manhattan (38.6). High-volume groups such as American and Beverages &amp; Snacks score consistently well.
      <span class="note">Note: Higher score means worse performance.</span>
    </div>
    <figure>
      <div class="heat-row">
        <div class="heat-main">
          <div class="fig-frame">
            <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Heatmap of average inspection score by cuisine group (rows) and borough (columns). Redder cells indicate higher, worse average scores; South Asian and African rows are among the reddest.">
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="heat-legend">
          <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Gradient legend: yellow at the low end near score 10, deep red at the high end near score 38.">
          <div class="lgnd-note">Higher&nbsp;=&nbsp;worse</div>
        </div>
      </div>
      <figcaption><span class="fno">Fig 2</span>Average inspection score by cuisine group and borough. Colour runs from yellow (lower, better average score) to red (higher, worse). Grey cells indicate no or insufficient data for that combination.</figcaption>
    </figure>
  </section>

  <!-- Q3 -->
  <section class="q" id="q3">
    <div class="q-head">
      <div class="q-index">3</div>
      <div class="q-headtext">
        <p class="q-kicker">Trends · grades &amp; violations over time</p>
        <h2 class="q-title">How do restaurant grades and violations vary across boroughs and over time?</h2>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="finding">
      <span class="tag">Finding</span>
      Grade A rates are broadly stable to improving from 2022 to 2024, while violation <strong>10F</strong> rises year on year in every borough and <strong>08A</strong> consistently follows it. A sharp jump in <strong>pending / not-yet-graded</strong> statuses in 2025 reflects the partial year, as the dataset was extracted in September 2025 and covers roughly three quarters of it.
    </div>
    <div class="tabs" role="tablist" aria-label="Question 3 charts">
      <button class="tab-btn" role="tab" aria-selected="true" aria-controls="p3a" id="t3a">Grades incl. pending</button>
      <button class="tab-btn" role="tab" aria-selected="false" aria-controls="p3b" id="t3b">Grades A / B / C</button>
      <button class="tab-btn" role="tab" aria-selected="false" aria-controls="p3c" id="t3c">Violations over time</button>
    </div>

    <div class="tab-panel active" id="p3a" role="tabpanel" aria-labelledby="t3a">
      <figure>
        <div class="fig-frame"><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Small-multiple line charts per borough showing grade A, B, C and pending percentages from 2022 to 2025. Pending rises steeply in 2025."></div>
        <figcaption><span class="fno">Fig 3a</span>Grade trends 2022 to 2025 by borough, including pending / not-yet-graded status. The 2025 figures are year-to-date and the pending spike reflects the partial extract.</figcaption>
      </figure>
    </div>
    <div class="tab-panel" id="p3b" role="tabpanel" aria-labelledby="t3b">
      <figure>
        <div class="fig-frame"><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Small-multiple line charts per borough showing only grade A, B and C percentages from 2022 to 2025, with pending excluded."></div>
        <figcaption><span class="fno">Fig 3b</span>Grade trends 2022 to 2025 by borough, restricted to issued A, B and C grades. With pending statuses removed, grade A is broadly stable across the window.</figcaption>
      </figure>
    </div>
    <div class="tab-panel" id="p3c" role="tabpanel" aria-labelledby="t3c">
      <figure>
        <div class="fig-frame"><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Small-multiple line charts per borough showing the percentage rate of the top violation codes over time. 10F is highest throughout and rising."></div>
        <figcaption><span class="fno">Fig 3c</span>Top 5 violations over time by borough, 2022 to 2025, as a share of violations. 10F (black) leads in every borough and trends upward; 08A (red) consistently follows.</figcaption>
      </figure>
    </div>

    <div class="decode">
      <h3>Violation code reference</h3>
      <p class="lead">Descriptions for the violation codes appearing in the trend charts above.</p>
      <table class="codes">
        <thead><tr><th>Violation code</th><th>Violation description</th></tr></thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr><td class="code">02B</td><td>Hot TCS food item not held at or above 140°F.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">02G</td><td>Cold TCS food item held above 41°F; smoked or processed fish held above 38°F; intact raw eggs held above 45°F; or reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) TCS foods held above required temperatures except during active necessary preparation.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">04L</td><td>Evidence of mice or live mice in establishment's food or non-food areas.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">06C</td><td>Food, supplies, or equipment not protected from potential source of contamination during storage, preparation, transportation, display, service or from customer's refillable, reusable container. Condiments not in single-service containers or dispensed directly by the vendor.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">06D</td><td>Food contact surface not properly washed, rinsed and sanitised after each use and following any activity when contamination may have occurred.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">08A</td><td>Establishment is not free of harborage or conditions conducive to rodents, insects or other pests.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">10B</td><td>Anti-siphonage or back-flow prevention device not provided where required; equipment or floor not properly drained; sewage disposal system in disrepair or not functioning properly. Condensation or liquid waste improperly disposed of.</td></tr>
          <tr><td class="code">10F</td><td>Non-food contact surface or equipment made of unacceptable material, not kept clean, or not properly sealed, raised, spaced or movable to allow accessibility for cleaning on all sides, above and underneath the unit.</td></tr>
        </tbody>
      </table>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- Q4 -->
  <section class="q" id="q4">
    <div class="q-head">
      <div class="q-index">4</div>
      <div class="q-headtext">
        <p class="q-kicker">Geographic targeting · neighbourhoods</p>
        <h2 class="q-title">Where should the city focus inspections, policies, or education to improve food safety?</h2>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="finding">
      <span class="tag">Finding</span>
      Risk concentrates in a small set of neighbourhoods, chiefly in the <strong>Bronx</strong> (Allerton-Pelham Gardens, Woodlawn-Wakefield, Fordham South) and <strong>Queens</strong> (Elmhurst-Maspeth, Flushing). Flushing alone carries over 6,000 inspections at an average score of 30.7, the largest single concentration of sustained poor performance in the dataset.
    </div>
    <figure>
      <div class="fig-frame">
        <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Map of NYC with bubbles marking the top 10 priority neighbourhoods, sized by risk and coloured by borough, each labelled with its average inspection score.">
      </div>
      <figcaption><span class="fno">Fig 4</span>Top 10 priority neighbourhoods by food safety risk. Bubble size reflects the scale of the concern and colour denotes borough; each bubble is labelled with its average inspection score. The clusters fall mainly across Queens and the Bronx, with several in Brooklyn.</figcaption>
    </figure>
  </section>

</main>

<footer>
  <div class="wrap">
    <p class="fineprint">NYC Restaurant Inspection Analysis · Static findings dashboard · Data extracted September 2025</p>
  </div>
</footer>

<div class="lb" id="lightbox" aria-hidden="true">
  <button class="lb-close" id="lbClose" aria-label="Close">&times;</button>
  <img src="" alt="" id="lbImg">
</div>

<script>
  (function(){
    var lb=document.getElementById('lightbox'),lbImg=document.getElementById('lbImg'),lbClose=document.getElementById('lbClose');
    document.querySelectorAll('.fig-frame img').forEach(function(im){
      im.addEventListener('click',function(){lbImg.src=im.src;lbImg.alt=im.alt;lb.classList.add('open');lb.setAttribute('aria-hidden','false');});
    });
    function close(){lb.classList.remove('open');lb.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true');lbImg.src='';}
    lb.addEventListener('click',function(e){if(e.target!==lbImg)close();});
    lbClose.addEventListener('click',close);
    document.addEventListener('keydown',function(e){if(e.key==='Escape')close();});
  })();
  (function(){
    var btns=document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn'),panels=document.querySelectorAll('.tab-panel');
    btns.forEach(function(b){
      b.addEventListener('click',function(){
        btns.forEach(function(x){x.setAttribute('aria-selected','false');});
        panels.forEach(function(p){p.classList.remove('active');});
        b.setAttribute('aria-selected','true');
        document.getElementById(b.getAttribute('aria-controls')).classList.add('active');
      });
    });
  })();
</script>
</body>
</html>
